title: Credential Compromise: Excessive Billing Address Diversity on Card History
narrative: Fraudster uses stolen card credentials across multiple accounts or checkout sessions, each time providing different billing addresses that pass AVS verification. The card number and CVV are correct, so individual transactions clear basic checks. However, aggregate card-counter features reveal the card has been associated with far more distinct billing addresses in the rolling window than any legitimate cardholder would accumulate. Legitimate cardholders typically use one or two billing addresses (home, work); seeing five or more distinct addresses in recent history indicates the card is being tested or exploited across multiple fraudulent setups. The per-transaction amount and velocity appear normal, masking the compromise until card-level aggregation features expose the pattern.
indicators: C11 > 27 (p90), indicating card associated with excessive distinct billing addresses in rolling window
C8 > 29 (p90), elevated count of transactions on this card in recent timeframe
card1_distinct_addr1 >= 5, card seen with five or more different billing addresses
C13 between 2 and 24 (p10-p90), moderate distinct merchant count showing breadth not depth
TransactionAmt between 50 and 200, amount within typical cardholder range
D1 = 0 or D1 > 180, device-card pairing is either first-time or stale indicating new context
indicator_explanations: Card has been linked to an unusually high number of different billing addresses recently
Card shows elevated transaction frequency in the recent window beyond normal use
This specific card has appeared with five or more distinct billing addresses in history
Card is being used across multiple merchants but not repeatedly at same locations
Individual transaction amount fits the cardholder's normal spending pattern to avoid detection
Device and card combination is either brand new or not recently paired together
summary: Stolen card credentials are being used with multiple different billing addresses that individually pass AVS checks, but card-level aggregation reveals the card has accumulated far more distinct billing addresses than legitimate cardholder behavior would produce.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders rarely associate their card with more than two or three billing addresses. Excessive billing address diversity combined with elevated card transaction counts indicates the card number is being exploited across multiple fraudulent accounts or checkout sessions, each providing different stolen or synthetic billing information.
